mission.title: istio: CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1
mission.description: CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify istio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your istio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n istio --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `Error connecting to 127.0.0.6:15002: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:0->127.0.0.6:15002: connect: connection refused`

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
### Is this the right place to submit this?

### Bug Description

We've just upgraded from 1.25.1 to 1.26.1 and have noticed that the Istio CNI repair mode does not seem to (always?) be working correctly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1
steps.description:
**Please provide a description of this PR:**

When IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.
```yaml
### Additional Information

istio-cni-node logs:
```

steps.title: Confirm CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n istio --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `Error connecting to 127.0.0.6:15002: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:0->127.0.0.6:15002: connect: connection refused` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
**Please provide a description of this PR:**

When IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.
codeSnippets:
**Please provide a description of this PR:**

When IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.
Fixes #56587
cc @leosarra 
Looks like the pod did not have any iptables at all and the repair logic ended up not applying new iptables rules because the existing ones were consistent with the desired state (which I doubt is empty). This is weird.
@hamishforbes can you provide the debug logs from the istio cni node agent, it may shed some light

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
